data.title: CZ tweeted in support of Siyuan: Donating money does not equate to being able to "command" the other party; burning BNB benefits token holders.
data.description: Regarding the previous decision by Happy-Sci creator Siyuan to destroy all donations, CZ (@cz_binance) posted on X that he "agrees with and supports Siyuan's approach." In his tweet, CZ stated that donating to others does not mean the recipient is obligated to act according to the donor's wishes; he also noted that Siyuan clearly did not want any association with the related memes; and that redirecting "donations" to YZiLabs or platform employees would increase the company's processing difficulties. Regarding the BNB destruction, CZ stated that it was not a waste, mentioning that BNB Chain recently destroys over $1 billion worth of BNB every quarter, resulting in a deflationary total supply and benefiting BNB holders; as a BNB holder, he thanked anyone who destroyed their BNB.

data.title: Yield suffers a major financial hit totaling $3.73 million
data.description:
Yield, a decentralized finance (DeFi) protocol, has lost $3. 73 million in trade. This was a result of extreme slippage, resulting in 3. 84 million GHO being exchanged for just 112,000 USDC. According to Perkshield, the transaction involved six different tokens and leveraged two DeFi platforms, including Uniswap V4 and Bancor. Several internal ETH transfers were executed […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/yield-suffers-major-hit-totaling-3-73-million/

data.title: XRP Is Back in the Conversation for 2026 — Here Is What Analysts Are Actually Watching
data.description:

Here is a comprehensive overview of what analysts are actually focusing on as they assess XRP's viability and prospects in the evolving landscape of cryptocurrency.

data.title: French regulator reports some crypto firms unresponsive as EU MiCA licence deadline nears
data.description:
A French regulator has revealed that 30% of crypto companies without a MiCA licence in France are unresponsive. There’s no communication on ​whether they intend to get the licence required under new EU ‌ rules or will cease operating by July. Stéphane Pontoizeau, executive director of the market intermediaries and market infrastructures supervision directorate at […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/french-regulator-reports-some-crypto-firms-unresponsive-as-eu-mica-licence-deadline-nears/
